en: Oh my goodness, how can I see through this?
Yep, that's me.
And that's my buddy Cisco's phone up in the air.
ko: 그리고 저건 제 친구 Cisco 폰이 공중에 날아가는 거예요.

en: And if you're wondering how we ended up in this situation, well, let's backtrack a little and see how it all began.
ko: 어떻게 이런 상황이 됐는지 궁금하시다면, 좀 되돌아가서 어떻게 시작됐는지 볼게요.

en: We're recording?
Ladies and gentlemen, welcome to Six Flags.
ko: 여러분, Six Flags에 오신 걸 환영합니다.

en: So I had this story to tell about Six Flags.
that I was gonna talk about, but I was like, you know what, let's drive two hours and actually enjoy the rides.
ko: 그걸 얘기하려고 했는데, 그냥 2시간 운전해서 직접 놀이기구 타자고 했어요

en: and make a bit of a vlog about it.
I personally love Six Flags, and I'm all about the rides.
ko: 저는 Six Flags를 진짜 좋아하고, 놀이기구 덕후예요.

en: But half of the fun is who you go with, because there are so many different people at Six Flags.
ko: 근데 재미의 절반은 누구랑 가느냐인데, Six Flags에는 정말 다양한 사람들이 있거든요.

en: The first are the ones who go with you, but don't get on any rides.
ko: 첫 번째는 같이 가놓고 놀이기구를 하나도 안 타는 사람이에요.

en: Like, what's the point of coming?
The second are the bag holders.
You know, they hold your bags while you go on the rides.
ko: 여러분이 놀이기구 타는 동안 가방을 들고 있는 사람이죠.

en: They play an excuse because they don't want to get on the rides. You know what I mean?
ko: 놀이기구 타기 싫어서 핑계를 대는 거예요. 무슨 말인지 알죠?

en: The third are the ones who are more thrilling than you.
ko: 세 번째는 나보다 더 스릴을 즐기는 사람이에요.

en: Crew, we should get on the Superman first.
But bro, that's the tallest ride.
We should get on something that's a little less extreme.
And is it really Six Flags if there's not a mess?
ko: 그리고 난장판이 없으면 Six Flags가 아니죠?

en: Like, you can see a kid vomiting, or an adult.
ko: 애가 토하는 걸 볼 수도 있고, 어른이 토하기도 하고.

en: There could be an ice cream spill, a food spill.
This one time I went with my buddy, and he didn't think well enough.
ko: 한번은 친구랑 갔는데, 그 친구가 생각이 좀 짧았어요.

en: because he ate so much food.
And then he got on the rides.
I'm like, dude, don't you want to wait? Nah, bro, I don't want to wait. Okay.
ko: 내가 야, 좀 기다리지 않을래? 했더니 아니, 안 기다려. 알겠어.

en: And 20 minutes later, we got on our first rollercoaster, and just know, there was a spill.
ko: 그리고 20분 후에 첫 번째 롤러코스터를 탔는데, 그냥 알아두세요, 쏟아졌습니다.

en: Anyways, you know what? Let's get on some rides.
Look at the Superman.
It's the first ride we're going to go on.
ko: 이게 우리가 첫 번째로 탈 놀이기구야. 아니야.

en: No, it's not. Yes, it is. Once we got to the park,.
we decided to start small and work our way up on the rides.
ko: 작은 것부터 시작해서 점점 올라가기로 했어요.

en: Do you think they'll let me in?
I don't know, maybe.
ko: 모르겠어, 아마? 나 키 되는데, 키 되는데.

en: I'm tall enough, I'm tall enough. Barely.
We got this all to ourselves, bro.
Our private jet. Private jet.
Let's go. All right, can we go?
Let's go, Joby.
I'm going to be high.
Oh, dude, so that's ice.
I almost felt like I was going to fall.
ko: 떨어질 뻔한 기분이었어. 이제 찻잔 타러 가요.

en: So we're about to go on the teacups. Hot teacups.
Nice teacups. Oh my God.
Oh my God. Oh my God.
Oh my God.
Dude, it was so beautiful.
I feel so sick right now. That was insane.
ko: 지금 속이 너무 안 좋아. 미쳤다.

en: Once we were done with some small rides, we decided to go to the water park.
ko: 작은 놀이기구 몇 개 타고 나서, 워터파크로 가기로 했어요.

en: Yeah. You know where we at? We at the Hurricane.
So this is my channel.
This is my channel.
I've never liked the dress rooms because they're just mad dark for no reason.
ko: 탈의실은 항상 이유 없이 엄청 어두워서 싫어했어요.

en: All right, we're about to change in three, two, done.
ko: 좋아, 셋, 둘, 끝. 옷 갈아입었어.

en: Yo. Okay, enter.
Hey. Cool transition, huh? Can they see you?
My hair's so bad.
Look what yours is.
We didn't get any footage of us on the water slides or in the water.
ko: 워터슬라이드 타거나 물에서 놀 때 영상을 못 찍었어요.

en: We decided to keep our phones in the lockers and not lose them.
ko: 폰을 잃어버리지 않게 사물함에 넣어두기로 했거든요.

en: You saw what happened at the beginning of this video.
ko: 영상 처음에 무슨 일이 있었는지 봤잖아요.

en: It gets crazier.
However, beforehand, your boy made a friend.
ko: 근데 그 전에, 저 친구를 사귀었어요.

en: Okay, so we just came from the water park,.
we're kind of wet, but look who I found.
ko: 좀 젖어 있는데, 누굴 찾았는지 봐요. Tweety다.

en: It's Tweety. I'm so happy.
I've never seen a character here at Six Flags. This is so cool.
ko: Six Flags에서 캐릭터를 본 적이 없었어요. 이거 진짜 멋져.

en: We then decided to get some food. $20 chicken tenders.
ko: 그다음 뭘 좀 먹기로 했어요. 20달러짜리 치킨텐더.

en: $25 burger, I bet, right?
No, that was not that much.
And your boy was getting lucky with those character pictures.
ko: 그리고 저는 캐릭터 사진에 운이 좋았어요.

en: Dude, there's more characters.
Hi. Can I get a picture with you? Yeah, sure.
ko: 같이 사진 찍을 수 있어요? 네, 물론이죠.

en: Yeah, I thought you were filming.
And ladies and gentlemen, it was then time to get on the big rides.
ko: 여러분, 이제 대형 놀이기구를 탈 시간이었습니다.

en: And I forgot to mention that we bought the flash pass that day, which made us cut the line at the majority of the rides,.
ko: 그리고 그날 플래시 패스를 샀다는 걸 말하는 걸 깜빡했는데, 대부분의 놀이기구에서 줄을 안 서도 됐어요.

en: because time is money.
One of the first rides we decided to do was Scream.
ko: 처음으로 타기로 한 놀이기구 중 하나가 Scream이었어요.

en: You know, it's that ride that's a tower that goes up and down.
ko: 타워가 위아래로 움직이는 그 놀이기구 있잖아요.

en: And your boy had the genius idea of micing himself up while Cisco was filming from the floor.
ko: 그리고 저는 기가 막힌 아이디어로 마이크를 달고 Cisco는 아래서 촬영했어요.

en: You ready? Not really, no.
I'm just gonna be randomly talking right now.
First time I've ever been on this.
Are you guys think you're gonna scream or nah?
ko: 여러분 소리 지를 것 같아요, 아닌가요?

en: No.
So tell me something that you've done that is very crazy and that you absolutely regret.
ko: 그러면 엄청 미친 짓이었고 완전 후회하는 거 하나 말해봐요.

en: I'm really nervous right now. All right.
So let me tell you guys something real quick that happened today.
We decided to go on Six Flags, it was a very last minute trip.
ko: Six Flags 가기로 했는데, 완전 급하게 정한 거였어요.

en: And now I'm making myself up.
just so I can talk to you guys and just talk to you about the mic.
And oh my God!
Oh my God, I'm trying to...
Oh my God.
Oh, this is fun.
This is really cool.
It's not bad. Oh, it's over?
That was it, bro?
Dang.
It's not too bad.
That's cool.
Anyways, we did that. Very last minute plans.
ko: 아무튼, 그렇게 했어요. 완전 급하게 정한 계획.

en: Yeah, I got you, bro. All right, let's do it.
Another ride we decided to go on was the one and only Superman.
ko: 우리가 타기로 한 또 다른 놀이기구는 바로 Superman이었어요.

en: It's so loud.
I was planning on going with Cisco on this one, but the nerves were getting to him.
ko: Cisco랑 같이 타려고 했는데, 긴장이 되나 봐요.

en: I've been on this twice, but I'm still nervous.
ko: 이거 두 번 탔는데, 아직도 긴장돼.

en: We're doing front, okay?
Yeah, I don't know why I wanted to do front row for this one.
ko: 응, 왜 이걸 앞줄에서 타겠다고 했는지 모르겠어.

en: I feel numb right now.
ko: 지금 멍한 기분이야. 앞줄에 탈 수 있을 것 같지 않아.

en: I don't feel like I'm gonna be in the front. I feel nervous.
Are you nervous? Hold my hand.
And at the end, Cisco did not wanna get on anymore.
ko: 결국, Cisco는 더 이상 타기 싫다고 했어요.

en: All right, all right.
Okay, so I guess I'm going by myself and Cisco's gonna record me.
ko: 그러면 나 혼자 타고 Cisco가 찍어줄게.

en: The people behind me were asking where he went. Like, you're bugging out.
ko: 뒤에 있는 사람들이 걔 어디 갔냐고 물어봤어요. 너 지금 겁먹은 거지.

en: No, I'm not doing that, no. This kid's on psycho, yo.
ko: 아니, 나 안 타, 아니. 이 녀석 미쳤어.

en: And whoo, that ride, I felt like I was flying.
ko: 와, 그 놀이기구, 나는 것 같은 기분이었어.

en: Guys, I would never buy a picture in an amusement park, but this time around, I bought it.
ko: 여러분, 놀이공원에서 사진 안 사는데, 이번에는 샀어요.

en: I'm gonna hang it up.
And if you think I'm crazy, well, you're probably right,.
ko: 제가 미쳤다고 생각하시면, 맞을 수도 있어요.

en: because I saw how good those shots looked, so I decided to mic myself up and go on it again front row.
ko: 촬영 영상이 너무 좋아 보여서, 마이크 달고 다시 앞줄에서 타기로 했거든요.

en: I'm an idiot.
Anyways, what is something that you've always wanted to do?
ko: 아무튼, 항상 해보고 싶었던 게 뭐예요?

en: For me, it's being front row in the Superman.
My boy Cisco, he bailed out, but it's okay.
It doesn't really matter.
And honestly, at the same time, life is like a rollercoaster.
There's a bunch of ups and there's a bunch of downs.
ko: 올라갈 때도 있고 내려갈 때도 많죠.

en: I'm a bit of an extreme person.
because I really didn't think straight and now I'm gonna be front row in this Superman.
ko: 제대로 생각을 안 했는데 이제 Superman 앞줄에 앉아 있어요.

en: And I'm trying to act calm. I'm trying to tell a story right now.
ko: 침착한 척하고 있어요. 지금 이야기를 해보려고요.

en: And hopefully you guys can still hear me.
It's really quiet. It's really nice. You know what this feels like?
ko: 진짜 조용하다. 진짜 좋다. 이게 뭐 같은지 알아요?

en: This feels like a POV from one of those New England Six Flags videos.
ko: 뉴잉글랜드 Six Flags 영상에서 보던 1인칭 시점 같은 느낌이에요.

en: But actually, this is my second time riding the Superman because right now, I am almost close to the drop.
ko: 사실 Superman 두 번째 타는 건데, 지금 거의 드롭 지점에 가까워지고 있어요.

en: This drop is actually really insane.
I'm just gonna try to do a story time right now as we're going up.
Oh boy.
And life is like a rollercoaster because there's a lot of ups and there are a lot of drops.
ko: 인생은 롤러코스터 같아요, 올라갈 때도 많고 떨어질 때도 많으니까.

en: Oh!
Woo!
I was able to do this as a little kid.
ko: 어릴 때 이거 탈 수 있었는데.

en: Oh my goodness.
I'm trying to see if Cisco can still see me.
ko: Cisco가 아직 나를 볼 수 있는지 확인하려고 해.

en: I don't know if anyone can still hear me.
And the minute the ride was over, I felt a rush.
However, I felt like something was missing.
ko: 근데, 뭔가 빠진 것 같은 느낌이 들었어요.

en: Excuse me, man. I think I dropped my phone.
You didn't tell me there's no phone there?
Like on your seat or anything?
I swear to you.
Yep, a phone was lost on the Superman ride.
And it actually wasn't my phone.
It was my buddy Cisco's phone.
And you're probably wondering, Jorge, how could Cisco lose his phone if he wasn't even on the ride?
ko: Cisco가 놀이기구를 안 탔는데 어떻게 폰을 잃어버리냐고 궁금하시죠?

en: Well, I gave him my phone to record the footage because my mics were already hooked up to my phone.
ko: 제 마이크가 이미 제 폰에 연결돼 있어서, 촬영하라고 제 폰을 줬거든요.

en: But I still needed the flash pass to get through the line, so he gave me his phone.
ko: 근데 줄을 서려면 플래시 패스가 필요해서, Cisco가 자기 폰을 줬어요.

en: And I just decided to hold onto his phone in my pocket.
ko: 그래서 주머니에 Cisco 폰을 넣고 있었어요.

en: And another thing too, we recorded a little bit with his phone.
so we lost some of that footage.
Which explains why I keep on coming back and forth in post.
ko: 그래서 편집에서 왔다 갔다 하는 거예요.

en: because I had to fill in the gaps.
So after the ride, we were on my phone going through my footage,.
ko: 놀이기구 내린 후에 제 폰으로 영상을 확인하고 있었는데,

en: seeing how much we still have left.
And speaking of footage, we got the exact moment where I lost his phone.
ko: 영상 얘기가 나왔으니, 폰을 잃어버린 정확한 순간이 찍혔어요.

en: Did you catch it? Let me go frame by frame.
That alone was worth losing the phone.
You literally can't make this stuff up, guys.
ko: 이건 진짜 꾸며낼 수가 없어요, 여러분.

en: I had a hard feeling in my heart.
I was like, this kid's gonna lose my phone.
ko: 이 녀석이 내 폰 잃어버릴 것 같다고.

en: My heart sank.
I'm just like, I took my pocket and I'm like, oh, where's this phone?
ko: 주머니를 만져보니까, 어, 폰이 어디 갔지?

en: Where's this phone?
The phone is definitely destroyed, but if we get it back from Lost and Found,.
ko: 폰은 확실히 박살 났겠지만, 분실물 센터에서 찾으면

en: we can get the footage.
ko: 영상은 건질 수 있어. 왜 이렇게 쿨한 거야?

en: Why was he so chill? I'm so confused.
The first thing I did tell him though was I'm gonna buy him a new phone.
ko: 제일 먼저 한 말은 새 폰 사줄게였어요.

en: And apparently he had all his stuff backed up on iCloud.
ko: 그리고 다행히 iCloud에 다 백업이 돼 있었대요.

en: So I guess we were just equally laughing at the footage.
ko: 그래서 둘 다 영상 보면서 웃었어요.

en: Shout out to Cisco though.
That's a real one for not getting mad.
Me and Lost and Found are trying to,.
we got a nice lady right now.
Is that what they look like when it comes to men?
Once we filed the Lost and Found claim, we decided to head back to the car.
ko: 분실물 신고를 하고 나서, 차로 돌아가기로 했어요.

en: We're back in the car. There's only one phone in this car.
ko: 차에 돌아왔어요. 이 차에 폰이 하나밖에 없어.

en: Only one phone and it's not mine.
We then went to get him a new phone.
We made it to the Best Buy.
This is a weird Best Buy at a mall.
All right, so they don't have the iPhone 12 Pro Max that he had, right?
ko: 그가 가지고 있던 iPhone 12 Pro Max가 없대, 맞지?

en: Yeah. They have the 13 Pro Max, so we're just gonna get him that one.
ko: 응. 13 Pro Max가 있어서 그걸로 사줄 거야.

en: It's a good upgrade, right?
However, getting the phone was harder than expected.
ko: 근데 폰 사는 게 생각보다 어려웠어요.

en: All right, so another plan two.
They had the phone directly with T-Mobile, which is what he has,.
ko: T-Mobile 통해서 폰이 있었는데, 걔가 쓰는 통신사인데,

en: but he's not an authorized user to actually buy the phone.
ko: 근데 폰을 살 수 있는 인가된 사용자가 아니래.

en: And legally he can't take it.
So we are going to Target, which is still open, thank God.
ko: 그래서 Target으로 가는데, 다행히 아직 영업 중이에요.

en: Target doesn't have unlocked iPhones.
It's great.
ko: 좋겠다. 그러면 내일 폰 사자, 알겠지?

en: All right, we'll just get your phone tomorrow then, right? Yeah.
So I don't know. What do we do, next day, transition?
ko: 그래서 모르겠다. 어떡하지, 다음 날, 전환?

en: No, I'm gonna do it.
Okay, now it's the next day, and yeah, I got his phone.
ko: 좋아, 다음 날이고, 네, 폰 샀어요.

en: Now I'm gonna go surprise him with it.
ko: 이제 서프라이즈로 줄 거야. 머리 아직 엉망이야.

en: My hair's still a mess. Your hair's still a mess?
I'm sorry. Here you go.
Oh my gosh, Collin.
That was such a fake reaction. Let's do it again.
ko: 그거 완전 가짜 리액션이잖아. 다시 하자.

en: Okay, next day, all right.
Why do you look sad?
Because that's the reaction. He's quiet. Oh!
Shut up! Hey, there we go.
Cling, cling, cling, cling, cling, cling.
ko: 짤랑, 짤랑, 짤랑, 짤랑, 짤랑, 짤랑. 인스타 팔로우.

en: Follow the Instagram. Follow the Instagram.
